extraction.one_line_summary: DÉMARE Arnaud won a messy sprint after Deceuninck-Quick-Step controlled final intermediate sprint for bonus seconds at 23km to go
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck-Quick-Step executed Tour de France-style intermediate sprint leadout at 23km out, prioritizing bonus seconds over stage positioning.
UAE and BahMcLaren shared early pace-setting responsibility at 86km out, revealing neither had clear sprint leadership.
Sagan launched sprint first from distance indicating lack of confidence in pure speed finish.

rider_signals.ULISSI Diego: positive: actively defended Maglia Ciclamino points at first intermediate sprint, showing GC riders can contest sprint jersey early
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: dropped on categorized climb with 70km remaining then fought back on descent, revealing marginal climbing capacity
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: dropped on climb with same group as Viviani, exposing weakness on even moderate gradients with 70km remaining
rider_signals.BANNING Peter: neutral: involved in crash at 86km that also took down Sepulveda but continued after medical check
